spec_match.rationale: Three persistent keys set once at bootstrap, attached to all subsequent crash payloads — canonical use of the per-Document map.

interactive.rationale: Editable text inputs for sha/release/cohort, an apply button, and a crash simulator that emits the resulting payload.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises a one-shot bootstrap pattern; the page delivers it with a sample SHA / release / cohort.

edge_cases.rationale: Doesn't show what happens if Apply is called multiple times with different values (overwrite vs append), or what the report looks like before any tags are set.

references.rationale: Page wraps but the see-also block (cut off in the read) needs a chromestatus link verified — if missing, that's an invariant 3 hit.

design_system.rationale: Variables only, .pill / .diff use the design system tokens, no static-card.

openQuestions.title: Demonstrate apply-twice semantics
openQuestions.detail: Press Apply with sha=A, then again with sha=B — does the second overwrite, or does the crash get both? The spec maps a key to a single value, but the demo should make that operational behaviour visible by stamping a timestamp and showing only the last-write-wins value.

openQuestions.title: Show a 'before tagging' baseline
openQuestions.detail: A crash simulated before pressing Apply currently shows an empty tags object. Pair that with the after-tagging payload as a side-by-side so visitors see exactly what value the feature delivers — currently it's implied.

openQuestions.title: Verify chromestatus reference link
openQuestions.detail: The see-also section continues past what's quoted; confirm it includes chromestatus.com/feature/6228675846209536 per invariant 3, and add if missing.

summary: Tight, well-scoped demo for the bootstrap pattern. Strengthen by showing the overwrite semantics and the before/after diff so the value proposition is concrete.
